• Main Page
  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

Bare Metal Environment APIs

Detailed Description

The bare metal environment provides a variety of APIs to help collect information from the booter and control the hardware.

Data Structures

struct  bme_memory_attr
 Structure to describe caching attributes of mapped memory. More...
struct  bme_saved_regs
 Caller-saved registers from the thread of execution that incurred an interrupt. More...
struct  bme_saved_regs_full
 All registers from the thread of execution that incurred an interrupt. More...
struct  bme_mem_seg_info
 Mapping information for a segment of memory. More...
struct  bme_free_mem_info
 Information for the free memory in the system. More...
struct  bme_extra_file_info
 Information for extra files in the system. More...
struct  bme_io_dev_info
 Information for a particular I/O device. More...
struct  bme_tile_info_t
 Information for a particular tile. More...
struct  bme_global_info_t
 Global information, passed to each tile. More...
struct  bme_local_info_t
 Local information, passed to each tile on its stack. More...
struct  tte_t
 The full data translation table entry. More...
union  pos_t
 Type for tile coordinates; matches that used by hardware in most contexts, such as when sending an IDN or UDN message. More...

Defines

#define SHIM_PROBE_TIMEOUT   4000
 Number of times cfg_probe() checks for a reply from an I/O shim before deciding it doesn't exist.
#define BME_MEMORY_MAP_FLAGS_WRITEABLE   1
 Memory mapping flags.
#define BME_CACHE_MODE_LOCAL   0
 Local cache mode.
#define BME_CACHE_MODE_HASH   1
 Hash-for-home cache mode.
#define BME_CACHE_MODE_NONE   2
 Do not cache.
#define BME_CACHE_MODE_COORDS   3
 Location override cache mode.
#define BME_CURRENT_VERSION_MAJOR   1
 The current BME Major version number.
#define BME_CURRENT_VERSION_MINOR   0
 The current BME Minor version number.
#define BME_VERSION_DEF(major, minor)   (((major) << 8) | (minor))
 A macro for defining the BME revision.
#define BME_VERSION_MAJOR(x)   (((x) >> 8) & 0xFF)
 Extract 'major' from a version number.
#define BME_VERSION_MINOR(x)   ((x) & 0xFF)
 Extract 'minor' from a version number.
#define BME_DRV_STILE_MAX   1
 Maximum number of shared tiles per instance.
#define BME_DRV_DTILE_MAX   7
 Maximum number of dedicated tiles per instance.
#define BME_MAX_IDN_PORTS   2
 Maximum number of IDN ports per shim.
#define BME_MAX_MDN_PORTS   3
 Maximum number of MDN ports per shim.
#define BME_MAX_MSHIMS   4
 Maximum number of memory shims.
#define BME_MAX_TILES   256
 Maximum number of tiles supported per client.
#define BME_MAX_MEM_SEGMENTS   (CHIP_ITLB_ENTRIES() + CHIP_DTLB_ENTRIES())
 Maximum number of memory segments, limited to match the number of TLB entries.
#define BME_MAX_NAME_LEN   32
 Maximum length of a character string used in BME data structures.
#define BME_TTE_INDEX_WIRED   -1
 Install the TTE in the next available unwired slot, then wire it.
#define BME_TTE_INDEX_RANDOM   -2
 Install the TTE in the unwired slot suggested by the hardware random replacement algorithm.
#define BME_LOAD_TLB(tlb, index)
 Set a TLB's index register, loading data into the xxx_CURRENT registers.
#define VPFN(va)   ((va) >> 12)
 Extract a virtual page frame number from a VA.
#define PFN_LO(pa)   (((pa) & 0xFFFFFFFF) >> 12)
 Extract low page frame number bits from a physical address.
#define PFN_HI(pa)   ((pa) >> 32)
 Extract high page frame number bits from a physical address.
#define TTE_PS_4K   0
 4K page size code
#define TTE_PS_16K   1
 16K page size code
#define TTE_PS_64K   2
 64K page size code
#define TTE_PS_256K   3
 256K page size code
#define TTE_PS_1M   4
 1M page size code
#define TTE_PS_4M   5
 4M page size code
#define TTE_PS_16M   6
 16M page size code
#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_4K   12
 4K page shift
#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_16K   14
 16K page shift
#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_64K   16
 64K page shift
#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_256K   18
 256K page shift
#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_1M   20
 1M page shift
#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_4M   22
 4M page shift
#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_16M   24
 16M page shift
#define TTE_SHIFT_TO_PS(x)   (((x) - 12) >> 1)
 Convert log2 of a page size to its page size code.
#define TTE_PS_TO_SHIFT(x)   (((x) << 1) + 12)
 Covert a page size code to log2 of the size of the page.

Typedefs

typedef struct bme_memory_attr bme_memory_attr_t
 Structure to describe caching attributes of mapped memory.
typedef int bme_dtlb_miss_handler_t (VA va)
 Typedef for miss handling function.
typedef void bme_interrupt_handler_t (int interrupt, struct bme_saved_regs_full *sr)
 Type for an interrupt routine.
typedef uint32_t bme_pa_pool_t
 Handle on allocated pool.
typedef struct bme_mem_seg_info bme_mem_seg_info_t
 Mapping information for a segment of memory.
typedef struct bme_free_mem_info bme_free_mem_info_t
 Information for the free memory in the system.
typedef struct bme_extra_file_info bme_extra_file_info_t
 Information for extra files in the system.
typedef struct bme_io_dev_info bme_io_dev_info_t
 Information for a particular I/O device.
typedef uint32_t Lotar
 Location override target.
typedef uint32_t Asid
 Address space identifier.
typedef unsigned long VA
 Virtual address.
typedef uint64_t PA
 Physical address.
typedef uint32_t bme_va_pool_t
 Handle on allocated pool.

Enumerations

  SEG_TYPE_UNKNOWN, SEG_TYPE_TEXT, SEG_TYPE_RODATA, SEG_TYPE_RWDATA,
  SEG_TYPE_TEXT_RODATA
}
 Memory segment types. More...
enum  bme_executable_type_t { EXEC_TYPE_UNKNOWN, EXEC_TYPE_HV, EXEC_TYPE_LINUX, EXEC_TYPE_BME }
 Executable types. More...

Functions

void * bme_malloc (size_t bytes)
 Allocate memory from the heap.
void bme_free (void *mem)
 Return memory refererenced by mem to the heap.
void bme_cfg_wr (pos_t dest, uint32_t chan, uint32_t addr, uint32_t data)
 Write to an I/O shim configuration register.
uint32_t bme_cfg_rd (pos_t dest, uint32_t chan, uint32_t addr)
 Read from an I/O shim configuration register.
int bme_cfg_probe (pos_t dest, uint32_t chan, uint32_t addr, uint32_t *rval)
 Probe an I/O shim configuration register.
int bme_default_dtlb_handler_init (void)
 Initialize and install the default DTLB miss handling system.
int bme_install_dtlb_handler (VA va, bme_dtlb_miss_handler_t *miss_handler)
 Install a DTLB miss handler.
int bme_uninstall_dtlb_handler (VA va)
 Uninstall a DTLB miss handler.
int bme_memory_map (VA va, PA pa, int page_size, int num_pages, bme_memory_attr_t *mem_attr, int wired)
 Create a memory mapping for the given VA, PA, number of pages, and page size with the given caching attributes.
int bme_memory_unmap (VA va, int size)
 Unmap the memory starting at the given VA.
void bme_dump_saved_regs (struct bme_saved_regs *sr)
 Dump out a set of saved registers.
void bme_dump_saved_regs_full (struct bme_saved_regs_full *sr)
 Dump out a full set of saved registers.
void bme_dump_dma_regs (void)
 Dump out the DMA engine's registers.
void bme_bad_intr (int int_number, struct bme_saved_regs_full *sr)
 Handler for a bad (unimplemented) interrupt.
void bme_base_intr (int int_number, struct bme_saved_regs_full *sr)
 Base interrupt handler, calls installed handler.
void bme_mask_interrupt (int interrupt)
 Mask an interrupt at PL2.
void bme_unmask_interrupt (int interrupt)
 Unmask an interrupt at PL2.
int bme_install_interrupt_handler (int interrupt, bme_interrupt_handler_t *func)
 Install an interrupt handler.
int bme_uninstall_interrupt_handler (int interrupt)
 Remove an interrupt handler by reinstalling the default.
int bme_create_pa_pool (PA start_pa, int page_size, int num_pages, bme_pa_pool_t *pa_pool)
 Create a pool of physical address space from which physical addresses can be allocated.
void bme_free_pa_pool (bme_pa_pool_t *pa_pool)
 Frees the storage occupied by a physical address pool.
int bme_alloc_pa_range (bme_pa_pool_t *pa_pool, int num_pages, PA *pa)
void bme_free_pa_range (bme_pa_pool_t *pa_pool, int num_pages, PA pa)
 Free num_pages of contiguous allocated PA range, starting at the page on which pa resides.
void * bme_get_pertile_state (void)
 Get this tile's state pointer.
void bme_get_pertile_state (void *state)
 Set this tile's state pointer.
bme_global_info_t * bme_map_global_info (void)
 Map the global information structure with a wired TTE, at the address suggested by the hypervisor, and return a pointer to the structure.
void bme_unmap_global_info (void)
 Decrements the reference count for the global information structure; if doing so causes the reference count to become zero, invalidates its mapping in the DTLB.
int bme_tile_index (void)
 Return the index of this tile in the BME global_information structure; that is, global_info.tile_table[bme_tile_index()] is this tile's entry in the tile table.
int bme_tile_ordinal (void)
 Return the ordinal number of this tile within the BME application; ranges from zero to the number of application tiles minus 1.
int bme_num_tiles (void)
 Return the number of tiles in this BME application.
void bme_dump_itlb (char *title)
 Dump the main processor instruction TLB.
void bme_dump_dtlb (char *title)
 Dump the data TLB.
void bme_clean_itlb (int clean_wired)
 Zero out the main processor instruction TLB.
void bme_clean_dtlb (int clean_wired)
 Zero out the data TLB.
int bme_pte2tte (VA va, PA pa, HV_PTE pte, int allow_incoherent, tte_t *ttep)
 Translate a physical address, virtual address, and a hypervisor PTE (as might be obtained via the Linux bme_mem driver) to a tte.
int bme_install_dtte (tte_t *ttep, int index)
 Install a new data TLB entry.
int bme_remove_dtte (int index)
 Remove a TLB entry, and, if it is the last wired entry, unwire it.
int bme_install_itte (tte_t *ttep, int index)
 Install a new instruction TLB entry.
int bme_remove_itte (int index)
 Remove a TLB entry, and, if it is the last wired entry, unwire it.
int bme_create_va_pool (VA start_va, int page_size, int num_pages, bme_va_pool_t *va_pool)
 Create a pool of virtual address space from which virtual addresses can be allocated.
void bme_free_va_pool (bme_va_pool_t *va_pool)
 Frees the storage occupied by a virtual address pool.
int bme_alloc_va_range (bme_va_pool_t *va_pool, int num_pages, VA *va)
void bme_free_va_range (bme_va_pool_t *va_pool, int num_pages, VA va)
 Free num_pages of contiguous allocated VA range, starting at the page on which va resides.

Variables

const char *const bme_int_names []
 Array of interrupt names, indexed by interrupt number.

Define Documentation

#define SHIM_PROBE_TIMEOUT   4000

Number of times cfg_probe() checks for a reply from an I/O shim before deciding it doesn't exist.

#define BME_MEMORY_MAP_FLAGS_WRITEABLE   1

Memory mapping flags.

Map memory as writeable

#define BME_CACHE_MODE_LOCAL   0

Local cache mode.

#define BME_CACHE_MODE_HASH   1

Hash-for-home cache mode.

#define BME_CACHE_MODE_NONE   2

Do not cache.

#define BME_CACHE_MODE_COORDS   3

Location override cache mode.

#define BME_CURRENT_VERSION_MAJOR   1

The current BME Major version number.

#define BME_CURRENT_VERSION_MINOR   0

The current BME Minor version number.

#define BME_VERSION_DEF ( major,
minor   )     (((major) << 8) | (minor))

A macro for defining the BME revision.

Parameters:
major Major revision number, new values signal incompatible changes.
minor Minor revision number, new values should be compatible.

#define BME_VERSION_MAJOR ( x   )     (((x) >> 8) & 0xFF)

Extract 'major' from a version number.

#define BME_VERSION_MINOR ( x   )     ((x) & 0xFF)

Extract 'minor' from a version number.

#define BME_DRV_STILE_MAX   1

Maximum number of shared tiles per instance.

#define BME_DRV_DTILE_MAX   7

Maximum number of dedicated tiles per instance.

#define BME_MAX_IDN_PORTS   2

Maximum number of IDN ports per shim.

#define BME_MAX_MDN_PORTS   3

Maximum number of MDN ports per shim.

#define BME_MAX_MSHIMS   4

Maximum number of memory shims.

#define BME_MAX_TILES   256

Maximum number of tiles supported per client.

#define BME_MAX_MEM_SEGMENTS   (CHIP_ITLB_ENTRIES() + CHIP_DTLB_ENTRIES())

Maximum number of memory segments, limited to match the number of TLB entries.

#define BME_MAX_NAME_LEN   32

Maximum length of a character string used in BME data structures.

#define BME_TTE_INDEX_WIRED   -1

Install the TTE in the next available unwired slot, then wire it.

Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

#define BME_TTE_INDEX_RANDOM   -2

Install the TTE in the unwired slot suggested by the hardware random replacement algorithm.

#define BME_LOAD_TLB ( tlb,
index   ) 

Value:

do \
  { \
    __insn_mtspr(SPR_ ## tlb ## TLB_INDEX, (index) | (1 << 31)); \
    asm("drain"); \
  } while (0)
Set a TLB's index register, loading data into the xxx_CURRENT registers.

FIXME: This works if the drain is replaced with a nop, but the hardware documentation doesn't tell you what's architecturally required; once it does this may change.

#define VPFN ( va   )     ((va) >> 12)

Extract a virtual page frame number from a VA.

#define PFN_LO ( pa   )     (((pa) & 0xFFFFFFFF) >> 12)

Extract low page frame number bits from a physical address.

#define PFN_HI ( pa   )     ((pa) >> 32)

Extract high page frame number bits from a physical address.

#define TTE_PS_4K   0

4K page size code

#define TTE_PS_16K   1

16K page size code

#define TTE_PS_64K   2

64K page size code

#define TTE_PS_256K   3

256K page size code

#define TTE_PS_1M   4

1M page size code

#define TTE_PS_4M   5

4M page size code

#define TTE_PS_16M   6

16M page size code

#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_4K   12

4K page shift

#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_16K   14

16K page shift

#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_64K   16

64K page shift

#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_256K   18

256K page shift

#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_1M   20

1M page shift

#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_4M   22

4M page shift

#define TTE_PG_SHIFT_16M   24

16M page shift

#define TTE_SHIFT_TO_PS ( x   )     (((x) - 12) >> 1)

Convert log2 of a page size to its page size code.

#define TTE_PS_TO_SHIFT ( x   )     (((x) << 1) + 12)

Covert a page size code to log2 of the size of the page.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct bme_memory_attr bme_memory_attr_t

Structure to describe caching attributes of mapped memory.

typedef int bme_dtlb_miss_handler_t(VA va)

Typedef for miss handling function.

Type for an interrupt routine.

typedef uint32_t bme_pa_pool_t

Handle on allocated pool.

typedef struct bme_mem_seg_info bme_mem_seg_info_t

Mapping information for a segment of memory.

typedef struct bme_free_mem_info bme_free_mem_info_t

Information for the free memory in the system.

typedef struct bme_extra_file_info bme_extra_file_info_t

Information for extra files in the system.

typedef struct bme_io_dev_info bme_io_dev_info_t

Information for a particular I/O device.

Location override target.

Address space identifier.

typedef unsigned long VA

Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

typedef uint32_t bme_va_pool_t

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum bme_seg_type_t

Memory segment types.

Enumerator:
SEG_TYPE_UNKNOWN  Unknown segment.

SEG_TYPE_TEXT  Executable instructions.

SEG_TYPE_RODATA  Read-only data.

SEG_TYPE_RWDATA  Read-write data.

SEG_TYPE_TEXT_RODATA  Executable instructions and read-only data.

enum bme_executable_type_t

Executable types.

Function Documentation

void* bme_malloc ( size_t  bytes  ) 

Allocate memory from the heap.

Parameters:
bytes Number of bytes to allocate.
Returns:
Pointer to allocated memory, 0 if none.

void bme_free ( void *  mem  ) 

Return memory refererenced by mem to the heap.

Parameters:
mem Pointer to memory to free.

void bme_cfg_wr ( pos_t  dest,
uint32_t  chan,
uint32_t  addr,
uint32_t  data 
)

Write to an I/O shim configuration register.

Parameters:
dest Destination. The FB bit is always set on the sent message.
chan Channel to target on the shim.
addr Register number within the channel.
data Data item to write.

uint32_t bme_cfg_rd ( pos_t  dest,
uint32_t  chan,
uint32_t  addr 
)

Read from an I/O shim configuration register.

Parameters:
dest Destination. The FB bit is always set on the sent message.
chan Channel to target on the shim.
addr Register number within the channel.
Returns:
Data item read.

int bme_cfg_probe ( pos_t  dest,
uint32_t  chan,
uint32_t  addr,
uint32_t *  rval 
)

Probe an I/O shim configuration register.

Tries to read the register, and times out if no response is received.

Parameters:
dest Destination. The Final Route Selection (FB) bit is always set on the sent message.
chan Channel to target on the shim.
addr Register number within the channel.
rval Pointer where the read value is written, if the return value is 1.
Returns:
1 if the register was successfully read, else 0.

int bme_default_dtlb_handler_init ( void   ) 

Initialize and install the default DTLB miss handling system.

Returns:
0 if successful, negative number if not.

int bme_install_dtlb_handler ( VA  va,
bme_dtlb_miss_handler_t *  miss_handler 
)

Install a DTLB miss handler.

Parameters:
va Virtual address of 16MB range for which to use this handler.
miss_handler Function for servicing DTLB misses in this 16MB range.
Returns:
0 if successful, negative number if not.

int bme_uninstall_dtlb_handler ( VA  va  ) 

Uninstall a DTLB miss handler.

Parameters:
va Virtual address of 16M range for which to uninstall handler.
Returns:
0 if successful, negative number if not.

int bme_memory_map ( VA  va,
PA  pa,
int  page_size,
int  num_pages,
bme_memory_attr_t *  mem_attr,
int  wired 
)

Create a memory mapping for the given VA, PA, number of pages, and page size with the given caching attributes.

The first call to this function sets the page size to be used for the 16MB range of VA space corresponding to this VA, beginning at a 16MB-aligned virtual address. Subsequent calls to this function must use the same page size if any of the memory overlaps an initialized range.

Parameters:
va Virtual address of range of memory to be mapped.
pa Physical address of range of memory to be mapped.
page_size Size of pages to be used for this range.
num_pages Number of pages to map for this range.
mem_attr Caching and memory attributes of this memory.
wired If non-zero, wire the entry in the DTLB.
Returns:
0 if successful, negative number if not.

int bme_memory_unmap ( VA  va,
int  size 
)

Unmap the memory starting at the given VA.

If entries are wired in the TLB, remove them.

Parameters:
va Virtual address of range of memory to be unmapped.
size Size of memory, in bytes, to unmap.
Returns:
0 if successful, negative number if not.

void bme_dump_saved_regs ( struct bme_saved_regs *  sr  ) 

Dump out a set of saved registers.

Parameters:
sr Pointer to the saved register structure.

void bme_dump_saved_regs_full ( struct bme_saved_regs_full *  sr  ) 

Dump out a full set of saved registers.

Parameters:
sr Pointer to the saved register structure.

void bme_dump_dma_regs ( void   ) 

Dump out the DMA engine's registers.

If the engine is active, we stop it before dumping, and restart it afterward.

void bme_bad_intr ( int  int_number,
struct bme_saved_regs_full *  sr 
)

Handler for a bad (unimplemented) interrupt.

Parameters:
int_number Interrupt number.
sr Pointer to saved registers from time of interrupt.

void bme_base_intr ( int  int_number,
struct bme_saved_regs_full *  sr 
)

Base interrupt handler, calls installed handler.

Parameters:
int_number Interrupt number.
sr Pointer to saved registers from time of interrupt.

void bme_mask_interrupt ( int  interrupt  ) 

Mask an interrupt at PL2.

Parameters:
interrupt Interrupt number.

void bme_unmask_interrupt ( int  interrupt  ) 

Unmask an interrupt at PL2.

Parameters:
interrupt Interrupt number.

int bme_install_interrupt_handler ( int  interrupt,
bme_interrupt_handler_t *  func 
)

Install an interrupt handler.

Parameters:
interrupt Interrupt number.
func Exception handling function.
Returns:
Zero if handler was successfully installed, otherwise an error code.

int bme_uninstall_interrupt_handler ( int  interrupt  ) 

Remove an interrupt handler by reinstalling the default.

Parameters:
interrupt Interrupt number.
Returns:
Zero if handler was successfully uninstalled, otherwise an error code.

int bme_create_pa_pool ( PA  start_pa,
int  page_size,
int  num_pages,
bme_pa_pool_t *  pa_pool 
)

Create a pool of physical address space from which physical addresses can be allocated.

Users are responsible for ensuring that the address range covered by the pool are available for use.

Parameters:
start_pa PA at which to start the PA pool. Actual start_pa of the created pool will be a page aligned value that is equal to or greater than the value of this parameter.
page_size Size of pages to be allocated from this pool. Must be a supported page size.
num_pages Number of pages this range will cover.
pa_pool Pointer to the resulting pool that is created.
Returns:
0 if successful, non-zero if pool could not be created.

void bme_free_pa_pool ( bme_pa_pool_t *  pa_pool  ) 

Frees the storage occupied by a physical address pool.

int bme_alloc_pa_range ( bme_pa_pool_t *  pa_pool,
int  num_pages,
PA *  pa 
)

Parameters:
pa_pool PA pool from which to allocate contiguous range of PA space.
num_pages Number of pages to allocate.
pa Pointer to the resulting start pa of the allocated range.
Returns:
0 if successful, non-zero if range could not be allocated.

void bme_free_pa_range ( bme_pa_pool_t *  pa_pool,
int  num_pages,
PA  pa 
)

Free num_pages of contiguous allocated PA range, starting at the page on which pa resides.

Parameters:
pa_pool Pool which contains this PA range.
num_pages Number of contiguous pages to return to free pool.
pa start PA of first page to return to free pool.

void* bme_get_pertile_state ( void   ) 

Get this tile's state pointer.

Returns:
State pointer pointer.

void bme_get_pertile_state ( void *  state  ) 

Set this tile's state pointer.

Parameters:
state New state pointer pointer.

bme_global_info_t* bme_map_global_info ( void   ) 

Map the global information structure with a wired TTE, at the address suggested by the hypervisor, and return a pointer to the structure.

If called after the structure has been mapped, it is not mapped again, but a reference count is incremented.

Returns:
Pointer to the global information structure, or NULL if there was no room in the TLB for its mapping.

void bme_unmap_global_info ( void   ) 

Decrements the reference count for the global information structure; if doing so causes the reference count to become zero, invalidates its mapping in the DTLB.

Note: if other wired TTEs have been added since the structure was mapped, this will leave an invalid but wired entry in the DTLB.

Returns:
Pointer to the global information structure.

int bme_tile_index ( void   ) 

Return the index of this tile in the BME global_information structure; that is, global_info.tile_table[bme_tile_index()] is this tile's entry in the tile table.

Returns:
Tile index.

int bme_tile_ordinal ( void   ) 

Return the ordinal number of this tile within the BME application; ranges from zero to the number of application tiles minus 1.

Returns:
Tile ordinal.

int bme_num_tiles ( void   ) 

Return the number of tiles in this BME application.

Returns:
Number of tiles.

void bme_dump_itlb ( char *  title  ) 

Dump the main processor instruction TLB.

Parameters:
title Optional title to print in dump header (can be NULL).

void bme_dump_dtlb ( char *  title  ) 

Dump the data TLB.

Parameters:
title Optional title to print in dump header (can be NULL).

void bme_clean_itlb ( int  clean_wired  ) 

Zero out the main processor instruction TLB.

Parameters:
clean_wired Iff nonzero, zero out the wired TLB entries.

void bme_clean_dtlb ( int  clean_wired  ) 

Zero out the data TLB.

Parameters:
clean_wired Iff nonzero, zero out the wired TLB entries.

int bme_pte2tte ( VA  va,
PA  pa,
HV_PTE  pte,
int  allow_incoherent,
tte_t *  ttep 
)

Translate a physical address, virtual address, and a hypervisor PTE (as might be obtained via the Linux bme_mem driver) to a tte.

Parameters:
va Virtual address to use.
pa Physical address to use. Note that the PFN contained within the actual PTE itself is ignored by this routine.
pte Page table entry to use.
allow_incoherent If nonzero, allow us to create a mapping that will not be coherent with the mapping on the original Linux tile that supplied the PTE. Normally this will result in an error return.
ttep Pointer to the returned tte.
Returns:
0 if the tte was successfully produced; nonzero if there was some error within the PTE, or if it would produce an incoherent mapping and allow_incoherent is zero.
Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

int bme_install_dtte ( tte_t *  ttep,
int  index 
)

Install a new data TLB entry.

Parameters:
ttep The DTLB entry to be installed.
index The index of the entry to be overwritten; or BME_TTE_INDEX_WIRED to use the next unwired entry, that will then be wired; or BME_TTE_INDEX_RANDOM to use the index suggested by the hardware random replacement algorithm.
Returns:
The TLB index used on success, or -1 if the requested entry cannot be used (either it is too large for the TLB, or BME_TTE_INDEX_WIRED was specified and no more wired entries are available).
Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

int bme_remove_dtte ( int  index  ) 

Remove a TLB entry, and, if it is the last wired entry, unwire it.

Returns:
0 on success, non-zero if the requested entry was wired, but not the last wired entry; in this case the entry will still be removed but will not be unwired.

int bme_install_itte ( tte_t *  ttep,
int  index 
)

Install a new instruction TLB entry.

Parameters:
ttep The ITLB entry to be installed.
index The index of the entry to be overwritten; or BME_TTE_INDEX_WIRED to use the next unwired entry (which will then be wired); or BME_TTE_INDEX_RANDOM to use the index suggested by the hardware random replacement algorithm.
Returns:
The TLB index used on success, or -1 if the requested entry cannot be used (either it's too large for the TLB, or BME_TTE_INDEX_WIRED was specified and no more wired entries are available).

int bme_remove_itte ( int  index  ) 

Remove a TLB entry, and, if it is the last wired entry, unwire it.

Returns:
0 on success, non-zero if the requested entry was wired, but not the last wired entry; in this case the entry will still be removed but will not be unwired.

int bme_create_va_pool ( VA  start_va,
int  page_size,
int  num_pages,
bme_va_pool_t *  va_pool 
)

Create a pool of virtual address space from which virtual addresses can be allocated.

Users are responsible for ensuring that the address range covered by the pool are available for use.

Parameters:
start_va VA at which to start the VA pool. Actual start_va of the created pool will be a page aligned value that is equal to or greater than the value of this parameter.
page_size Size of pages to be allocated from this pool. Must be a supported page size.
num_pages Number of pages this range will cover.
va_pool Pointer to the resulting pool that is created.
Returns:
0 if successful, non-zero if pool could not be created.

void bme_free_va_pool ( bme_va_pool_t *  va_pool  ) 

Frees the storage occupied by a virtual address pool.

int bme_alloc_va_range ( bme_va_pool_t *  va_pool,
int  num_pages,
VA *  va 
)

Parameters:
va_pool VA pool from which to allocate contiguous range of VA space.
num_pages Number of pages to allocate.
va Pointer to the resulting start va of the allocated range.
Returns:
0 if successful, non-zero if range could not be allocated.

void bme_free_va_range ( bme_va_pool_t *  va_pool,
int  num_pages,
VA  va 
)

Free num_pages of contiguous allocated VA range, starting at the page on which va resides.

Parameters:
va_pool Pool which contains this VA range.
num_pages Number of contiguous pages to return to free pool.
va start VA of first page to return to free pool.

Variable Documentation

const char* const bme_int_names[]

Array of interrupt names, indexed by interrupt number.
